sections.paragraph:
εὐσεβῶς adv. of εὐσεβής (Pind., X., Pla. et al.; ins, pap, LXX) in our lit. of one’s relation to God in a godly manner (oft. ins [SIG index]; 4 Macc 7:21 v.l.; EpArist 37 and 261; Philo, Aet. M. 10; Ath. 1:3 εὐσεβέστατα διακειμένους) 1 Cl 61:2 (cp. X., Mem. 4, 3, 16: ‘Can there be any better or more reverent way to honor the gods than by doing what they command?’); ζῆν 2 Ti 3:12; Tit 2:12 (s. βιοῦν εὐ.: Ps.-Pla., Axioch. 372a). W. δικαίως (SIG 772; Jos., Ant. 8, 300; ABurk, Die Pädagogik des Isokrates … ’23, 173 n. 2) 1 Cl 62:1. W. δικ. and σωφρόνως (Socrat., Ep. 15, 1 ἐβίωσε σωφρόνως κ. ὁσίως κ. εὐ.) Tit 2:12.—M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

oft. oft. = often

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 413
